CASE-15 Active Investigation

MEGA Account Compromise — Credential Stuffing via 3xK Tech Proxy Infrastructure

Opened 19 Jun 2026 - 14:25 · Updated 19 Jun 2026 - 16:19 · 19 Jun 2026 14:44 UTC
0.71
Evidence Weight
6
Pinned Signals
0
Linked Actors
0.92
Peak Signal
Download this case brief as PDF
Case Summary
Unauthorized access to personal MEGA cloud storage account detected 2026-06-19 15:53 SAST. Attacker used automated Go HTTP client from datacenter IP 209.50.171.190 (3xK Tech GmbH, AS200373, Ashburn VA). Infrastructure profile indicates a proxy cluster node with extensive abuse history. Attack vector assessed as credential stuffing — password reuse from a prior breach of another service.
Analyst Hypothesis
Credentials were obtained from a third-party data breach and tested against MEGA via automated tooling running on 3xK Tech GmbH proxy infrastructure. The attacker's goal is likely data exfiltration from MEGA cloud storage. Secondary hypothesis: the compromised credentials may be in active circulation on credential markets.

Methodology
All signals sourced from open-source media, public records, and official statements. Evidence weight is derived from signal gravity scores (urgency × source credibility × recency).
